macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: U.S. Adds 178,000 Jobs in March, Triple Economist Forecasts
items.summary: The U.S. economy added 178,000 jobs in March, far exceeding expectations after February losses, with unemployment dipping to 4.3% and hourly earnings up 3.5% year-over-year. President Trump credited his policies, including tariffs, while the data suggests the Federal Reserve may hold rates steady amid persistent inflation.

items.title: Strategist Rejects U.S. Recession Fears Despite Iran Conflict Volatility
items.summary: Carson Group's Ryan Detrick states the U.S. economy shows no recession signs, citing strong corporate earnings and improving manufacturing surveys, even as Iran war disrupts oil supply. Prolonged Strait of Hormuz closure could pressure Fed policy and heighten inflation risks.

items.title: Iran Air Strikes Trigger Oil Surge to Near $100 and Four-Week Stock Slump
items.summary: Energy market turmoil from air strikes on Iran has driven WTI oil prices toward $100 per barrel due to supply disruption fears, leading to a four-week decline in stocks amid heightened geopolitical uncertainty.

items.title: Expert Warns of Potentially Difficult Monday for Markets Amid Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: SlateStone's Kenny Polcari highlights investor responses to geopolitical uncertainty, predicting a challenging market open on Monday due to ongoing global risks.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Crypto Market Liquidations Reach $459 Million in 24 Hours, Long Positions Hit Hardest
items.summary: The cryptocurrency market saw $459 million in liquidations over the past 24 hours, with $270 million from long positions and significant impacts on Bitcoin ($98M) and Ethereum ($102M). A single $17.28 million liquidation occurred on Hyperliquid's SP500-USD pair amid high volatility.

items.title: Top Crypto Gainers: ONG Surges 38.39%, BLUR Up 40.30% in 24 Hours
items.summary: Ontology Gas (ONG) led gainers with a 38.39% rise to $0.0911 on $32.86M volume, while BLUR surged 40.30% to $0.0243 with $99.24M volume; other notables include YB (+28.71%), NOM (+27.58%), and EDGE (+18.87% to +50.88%). High volumes suggest strong market interest.

items.title: Major Crypto Losers: STO Plummets 33.47%, NOM Drops 29.05% Amid High Volume Sell-Offs
items.summary: STO fell 33.47% to $0.1501 on $945.6M volume, NOM dropped 29.05% to $0.0059 with $230.68M volume, indicating intense selling pressure and potential capitulation.

items.title: Bitcoin Trades at $66,600 After 47-50% Crash from ATH, Cathie Wood Remains Optimistic
items.summary: Bitcoin is down 47% from its $126,080 ATH to around $66,600-$66,938, following a flash crash; Cathie Wood calls it a 'real victory' compared to past 85-95% drops, emphasizing institutionalization and low correlation with other assets.

items.title: Bloomberg Strategist Warns of New Bitcoin Crash, Potential Return to $10,000
items.summary: Bitcoin, down 2.5% to $66,938 in 24 hours, faces crash risks with Bloomberg strategist forecasting a possible drop back to $10,000 amid heightened volatility.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Microsoft Launches Three New In-House AI Models Targeting OpenAI and Google
items.summary: Microsoft released three foundational AI models built entirely in-house, including a state-of-the-art speech transcription system, positioning it as a direct competitor to OpenAI and Google. The launch occurred on Thursday, April 3, 2026.

items.title: Arcee Releases Open-Source Trinity-Large-Thinking Model for Enterprises
items.summary: Arcee unveiled Trinity-Large-Thinking, a powerful U.S.-made open-source AI model that enterprises can download and customize, continuing the trend of accessible AI advancements.

items.title: OpenAI Acquires Tech Talk Show TBPN in First Media Acquisition
items.summary: OpenAI purchased TBPN, Silicon Valley's popular tech-focused talk show, to drive AI conversations, rooted in long-standing ties with the founder. Multiple reports confirm this as OpenAI's initial foray into media.
items.source: Silicon Republic, Ars Technica, The Next Web

items.title: Microsoft Plans $13 Billion Investment in Japan for AI and Cyber Defense
items.summary: Microsoft announced a $13 billion investment in Japan focused on AI and cyber defense expansion, boosting Sakura Internet's stock by 20%. This underscores growing AI infrastructure investments in Asia.

items.title: IBM and Arm Collaborate on Dual-Architecture Systems for AI Workloads
items.summary: IBM partnered with Arm to develop dual-architecture hardware for enterprise AI and data-intensive workloads, enhancing flexibility, reliability, and security.

items.title: Federal Reserve Reports 41% Work-Related Generative AI Adoption in US
items.summary: The U.S. Federal Reserve noted that 41% of individuals report using generative AI at work as of November 2025, with 78% of the labor force at AI-adopting firms and 54% at LLM-using ones, indicating rapid industry trends.

items.title: Take-Two Lays Off AI Team Including Head of Artificial Intelligence
items.summary: Take-Two Interactive laid off an unspecified portion of its AI team, including the Head of AI, amid industry restructuring.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Crimson Desert Dataminers Uncover Hidden Survival Mechanics
items.summary: Dataminers discovered unfinished survival-type endgame mechanics in Crimson Desert, hinting at potential future updates or modder implementations. Pearl Abyss is currently working on the next patch addressing known issues.

items.title: Alone in the Dark Creator Crowdfunding Mega Drive Game with Unique Spinner Controller
items.summary: Frédérick Raynal is crowdfunding PopCorn: Duel, a new Mega Drive/Genesis game featuring its own custom spinner controller.

items.title: Star Fox Rumored for Switch Release This Year Following Mario Galaxy Movie Cameo
items.summary: Leaker Nate the Hate claims Star Fox is coming to Switch this year as a big game, sparked by Fox McCloud's appearance in the Super Mario Galaxy Movie, amid mixed leak accuracy.

items.title: State of Decay 3 Opens Alpha Playtest Sign-Ups with Playtester Impressions
items.summary: Undead Labs has opened sign-ups for State of Decay 3 alpha playtest, with playtesters sharing positive impressions from recent sessions highlighting key developments.

items.title: Sea of Stars Port Heading to Mobile with Pre-Orders Live April 7
items.summary: Sea of Stars is coming to mobile platforms on April 7, with pre-orders now available digitally.

items.title: Dolphin Emulator Port Progressing for Nintendo Switch
items.summary: Developer Tico is working on porting the Dolphin emulator (GameCube/Wii) to Nintendo Switch, including Triforce Arcade support.

items.title: The Last of Us Online Director Learned of Cancellation Just 24 Hours Prior
items.summary: The director of the canceled The Last of Us Online multiplayer project revealed they were informed of the cancellation only 24 hours before public announcement.

items.title: Resident Evil Celebrates 30 Years with New Insights on Enduring Popularity
items.summary: Resident Evil marks its 30th anniversary, with producers discussing its genre-defining impact, zombie lore, and strategies for keeping the series fresh.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: AWS Zones in Dubai and Bahrain 'Hard Down' Due to Iranian Strikes
items.summary: Amazon's internal memo reveals that Iranian strikes have rendered two AWS zones in Dubai and Bahrain unavailable for an extended period, prompting employees to deprioritize these regions amid infrastructure damage in the Gulf.

items.title: Block Lays Off 4,000 Employees Citing AI Efficiency Gains
items.summary: Fintech company Block announced layoffs of more than 4,000 out of 10,000 staff due to efficiency gains from AI, causing shares to soar over 20% in after-hours trading.

items.title: NASA Allows Astronauts Smartphones on Artemis II Moon Mission
items.summary: Artemis II marks one of NASA's first times letting astronauts fly with modified iPhones for photos and videos during the moon mission.

items.title: Wipro Launches AI-Native Business Unit and Names New CEO
items.summary: Wipro launched a dedicated AI-native business and platforms unit amid leadership changes, appointing Nagendra Bandaru as CEO of the AI segment.

items.title: Oracle Conducts Layoffs Amid Rising AI and Data Center Spending
items.summary: Oracle trimmed its workforce, including Indian employees, as part of cost adjustments related to increased investments in AI and data centers.

items.title: Tesla Faces Escalated Federal Probe on Self-Driving Feature
items.summary: U.S. regulators expanded a probe into Tesla's self-driving feature after crashes, coinciding with plans to sell cars without steering wheels or pedals.

items.title: Product Design Startup Noon Emerges from Stealth with $44M Funding
items.summary: Noon, a product design startup, came out of stealth mode securing $44 million in funding to advance its operations.
